title: Button UI Component in Design Systems
content: # Button Systems Directories Components Articles Blueprints Guides Glossary Biweekly Digest Created by Accordion Action bar Action button Action list Alert banner App bar Autocomplete Avatar Badges Bottom sheet Breadcrumbs Button group Cards Checkbox Chip Code snippet Combobox Data table Date picker Dialog Divider Drawer Dropdown Empty state Filter input Form Help text Icon Inline message Label Link List Menus Modal Navigation bar Page header Pagination Popover Progress bar Progress indicator Radio button Search Segmented control Select Sidebar Slider Snackbar Switch Tabs Tag Text area Text field Tooltip Tree view Keep up with the latest in Design Systems Subscribe Button is a reusable, interactive UI element that triggers a specific action when clicked. As a fundamental element of user interaction, it serves as an imperative tool for navigation, form submission, and functionality initiation. Consistent design and implementation of buttons ensure intuitive user experience, reducing cognitive load. Teams employ them in different states and styles to denote importance and purpose. These pre-defined styles are applied across different parts of a software application or website, ensuring efficient, harmonious design throughout.
